casepack.title: Atomic Physics — 3 Representative Elements (Tier-2)
casepack.description: Atomic physics domain closure demonstrating GCD kernel applied to 3 elements spanning the periodic table. 8-channel trace with equal weights. Hydrogen (simplest atom, highest fidelity), Iron (d-block, moderate), Uranium (heaviest stable, Collapse). Verifies Tier-1 identities and demonstrates that atomic complexity correlates with drift.

run_intent.notes: Exercises the atomic physics closure with 3 elements: Hydrogen (Z=1 — simplest atom, highest F=0.844, Watch), Iron (Z=26 — d-block, moderate channels, Watch), Uranium (Z=92 — heavy element Collapse, many degraded channels push omega past 0.30). Heterogeneity gap tracks IC closely when curvature is low; Uranium shows structural degradation at high Z. Connects to cross-scale kernel (nuclear->atomic->bulk).
